title: Corticosteroids, Anti-Infectives, Vasodilators & Critical Care Agents Quiz

questions.text: What is the primary indication for inhaled corticosteroids?
Corticosteroids produce anti-inflammatory action in the lungs by inhibiting what?
What is the preferred route of corticosteroid administration for respiratory treatment?
Corticosteroids are contraindicated for what?
What fungal infection (yeast infection/Thrush) is a side effect of corticosteroid use?
What are two methods to prevent fungal infections from inhaled corticosteroids?

answers.text: Utilize a holding chamber and rinse mouth post treatment
Add a SABA to each treatment

questions.text: Advair (DPI & MDI) is a combination of which two medications?
What is the brand name for Budesonide?
What is the primary indication for nonsteroidal anti-asthma agents?

answers.text: Prophylactic management of mild persistent asthma

questions.text: Extrinsic or Atopic asthma triggers are caused by what?
What is the action of Cromolyn Sodium?

answers.text: Blocks IgE from attaching to mast cells
Encapsulates the cell membrane making breach more difficult, neutralizing release of inflammatory mediators

questions.text: Montelukast (Singulair) is indicated for prophylaxis and long-term treatment of asthma in patients of what age?
Which anti-leukotriene requires liver enzymes to be checked regularly due to liver damage susceptibility?
Omalizumab (Xolair) prevents what from attaching to mast cells?
Roflumilast (PDE4 inhibitor) increases what substance to suppress pro-inflammatory pathways?
Pentamidine (Nebupent) is indicated for prophylactic therapy for what condition?

answers.text: Pneumocystis Carinii Pneumonia (PCP) common in AIDS infected patients

questions.text: Ribavirin (Virazole) is indicated for infants with what condition?
What device is used to administer Ribavirin (Virazole)?
Tobramycin (Tobi) is indicated for what condition and is common treatment for CF patients?
Which of the following is NOT an inhaled negative effect of Tobramycin (Tobi)?
Zanamivir (Relenza) is indicated for influenza within the first how many days of infection?
Amphotericin B is indicated for what type of infections?
What type of antibiotics are Quinolones?

answers.text: Treat Gram+/- bacteria; a more broad-spectrum type of antibiotic

questions.text: Benadryl (Diphenhydramine) differs from Allegra, Zyrtec, and Claritin in what way?

answers.text: It is only available by prescription

questions.text: What is the primary FDA-approved indication for Inhaled Nitric Oxide (INOmax)?

answers.text: Persistent Pulmonary Hypertension in Neonates (PPHN) >34 wks

questions.text: What is the recommended dose for INOmax?
Methemoglobin is a risk of INOmax. What characterizes methemoglobin?

answers.text: A hemoglobin molecule with increased oxygen affinity
A ferric (Fe3+) hemoglobin molecule that cannot bind with oxygen
A hemoglobin molecule with reduced carbon dioxide binding

questions.text: Succinylcholine is what type of neuromuscular blocking agent?

answers.text: Depolarizing agent — the only agent in this category

questions.text: How quickly does Succinylcholine produce paralysis?
Does Succinylcholine have a reversal agent?
What is the reversal agent for non-depolarizing neuromuscular blocking agents?
Non-depolarizing agents are preferred for paralysis of what type of patients?
Which non-depolarizing agent has the greatest potential for cardiovascular side effects?
Effects of severe hyperkalemia from Succinylcholine include what?
Furosemide (Lasix) is the most commonly used diuretic for what condition?
What type of diuretic is Furosemide (Lasix)?
What is normal urine output?
Anuria is defined as what?

answers.text: No urine output (sign of renal failure)

questions.text: Hypotension is defined as blood pressure less than what?
Hypotension is NOT synonymous with what?
What are the two categories of shock?

answers.text: Hypo-dynamic (cardiogenic; hypovolemic) and hyper-dynamic (vascular; septic; anaphylactic)

questions.text: A colloid solution is described as what?

answers.text: Small molecule solution that crosses semipermeable membrane
Large molecule solution incapable of crossing the healthy semipermeable capillary membrane

questions.text: What is the most common crystalloid resuscitation fluid?
Depolarizing agents work by doing what to acetylcholine receptors?
Non-depolarizing agents work by doing what to acetylcholine receptors?
